channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The Trump administration is finding new ways to withhold federal funding for science
item.description: Billions in funding were cut under President Trump were restored in 2026. Now, watchdogs say the administration is finding other ways to withhold money – and scientists are feeling the consequences.
item.content:encoded: Billions in funding were cut under President Trump were restored in 2026. Now, watchdogs say the administration is finding other ways to withhold money – and scientists are feeling the consequences.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: The Trump administration is investigating Smith College for admitting trans women
item.description: Smith College is one of the largest women's colleges in the country. This week, the Trump administration announced it is investigating the school's practice of admitting trans women.
item.content:encoded: Smith College is one of the largest women's colleges in the country. This week, the Trump administration announced it is investigating the school's practice of admitting trans women.
item.dc:creator: Nirvani Williams

item.title: 'Transformation' of the U.S. Forest Service looks like dismantling to critics
item.description: In the month since the Trump administration announced a major reorganization of the U.S. Forest Service, critics have called it a stealth dismantling. The plan includes moving its headquarters.
item.content:encoded: In the month since the Trump administration announced a major reorganization of the U.S. Forest Service, critics have called it a stealth dismantling. The plan includes moving its headquarters.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Cohen

item.title: How doctors and abortion advocates are pivoting as mifepristone access shifts
item.description: Starting next Monday, doctors may not be able to mail people the abortion pill Mifepristone. That would increase barriers, but experts say it won't stop people's ability to get the pills in the mail.
item.content:encoded: Starting next Monday, doctors may not be able to mail people the abortion pill Mifepristone. That would increase barriers, but experts say it won't stop people's ability to get the pills in the mail.
item.dc:creator: Abby Wendle

item.title: Alabama lawmakers debate a congressional redistricting map
item.description: Alabama lawmakers are scrambling to change the state's congressional maps. The governor called a special session after the Supreme Court tossed a key provision of the Voting Rights Act.
item.content:encoded: Alabama lawmakers are scrambling to change the state's congressional maps. The governor called a special session after the Supreme Court tossed a key provision of the Voting Rights Act.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Elliott

item.title: From ICE detention to center stage: The Texas mariachi brothers opening for Kacey Musgraves
item.description: The brothers and their parents were taken into federal custody earlier this year during a required immigration check-in. The story caught the ear of a country music icon.
item.content:encoded:
The brothers and their parents were taken into federal custody earlier this year during a required immigration check-in. The story caught the ear of a country music icon.

(Image credit: Brenda Bazán for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: Activists aim to end Pakistan taxes on menstrual pads
item.description: A lawsuit in Pakistan challenges how period products are taxed. But sometimes that approach has not actually lowered prices.
item.content:encoded: A lawsuit in Pakistan challenges how period products are taxed. But sometimes that approach has not actually lowered prices.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: RFK Jr. wants people to taper off of antidepressants. Doctors urge caution
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is promoting new initiatives to wean people off of antidepressants like Zoloft and Prozac. Psychiatrists are urging caution before changing any medication.
item.content:encoded: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is promoting new initiatives to wean people off of antidepressants like Zoloft and Prozac. Psychiatrists are urging caution before changing any medication.
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: U.S. says it's made Hormuz safe for commercial shipping, but standoff hasn't changed
item.description: The administration has announced a new military strategy designed to open up the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping, but it's unclear if this will break Iran's grip on the key waterway.
item.content:encoded: The administration has announced a new military strategy designed to open up the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping, but it's unclear if this will break Iran's grip on the key waterway.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman
